_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Closed reduction of joint dislocation - Elbow
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years' experience
clinical_indications: The elbow joint is considered relatively stable; however, elbow dislocations are common occurrence.
Semilunar notch of the ulna is dislocated posteriorly from the distal humerus.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

claim.label: Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Detailed Clinical notes all vitals, history signs & symptoms, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advise for admission, and planned line of treatment?
Was clinical photograph of affected part submitted?
Was X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and X-ray report are not indicative of closed reduction of elbow joint dislocation.
Clinical notes are incomplete (missing vitals, history, signs, symptoms, physical examination, indication for procedure, planned management, or admission advice).
Clinical photograph of affected elbow joint is missing.
X-ray of affected elbow joint is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right).
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) are missing for the elbow joint dislocation claim.
Procedure/operative notes for elbow reduction are missing.
Post-procedure X-ray of the elbow joint is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, or side (Left/Right).
Post-procedure clinical photograph of the elbow joint is missing.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice for elbow joint dislocation is missing.

clinical_key_pointers: The elbow joint is considered relatively stable; however, elbow dislocations are common occurrence.
Semilunar notch of the ulna is dislocated posteriorly from the distal humerus.
